tldr: Note: Ably is pub/sub messaging and Daily is video/audio — this is not a like-for-like swap. Only migrate if your real Ably use case is voice/video rooms; otherwise reconsider the target.
why_switch: Daily is a fit if your underlying need is real-time audio/video calls, not generic pub/sub messaging
Lower entry pricing relative to Ably's $29/mo tier
Mature web and mobile SDKs with prebuilt UI
Single-vendor solution if you can drop pub/sub messaging entirely
what_to_export: List of channels and their configurations
Auth/token issuance logic from your backend
Webhook and integration rules configured in Ably
Presence usage patterns and member metadata schema
List of client apps using the Ably SDK
export_method: Use Ably's official dashboard and REST API to inventory channels, integration rules, and history settings. Treat your backend (token mint, message-producer code) as canonical business logic. Persisted channel history can be pulled via Ably's history API per their official docs. There is no documented one-click bulk export covering all configuration — script extraction and capture dashboard config manually.
import_method: Daily does not provide a generic pub/sub primitive — only proceed if your traffic on Ably is genuinely call/room oriented. Provision a Daily account, generate API keys, and implement server-side room creation and meeting token issuance against Daily's official REST API. Swap clients to Daily's per-platform SDKs. If you have non-call pub/sub workloads, plan a separate target for those (this guide does not cover that path).

concept_mapping.note: Conceptually different — only maps if the channel was used to coordinate a call
Both are short-lived backend-minted tokens; claim shapes differ entirely
Daily exposes participants in a call; not a general presence service

concept_mapping.target: Custom data / app messages on call
concept_mapping.note: Daily exposes per-call app messages; not a substitute for global pub/sub
Rewrite handler against Daily's event payload
Daily is not a message store; persist history yourself if needed

effort: 2-6 weeks (longer if your Ably usage is broader than calls)
gotchas: These are different product categories — confirm fit before starting
If you rely on persisted message history, presence at scale, or MQTT/SSE protocols, Daily is not a replacement
Token formats and capability models are not portable
Latency, fan-out and delivery-guarantee characteristics differ; load test before cutover
Pricing dimensions are different (messages/peers vs. participant minutes) — verify on Daily's official pricing page
Run both vendors in parallel during cutover and watch for dropped events

email.subject: Considering moving Ably real-time workloads to Daily
email.body:
Hi team,

Flagging a possible migration from Ably to Daily. Important caveat: Ably is pub/sub messaging and Daily is video/audio infrastructure, so this only makes sense if our real Ably workload is call/room coordination. For other pub/sub use cases we'd need a different target.

If the fit holds, scope is rebuilding server-side room creation and tokens against Daily's API, swapping clients, and reconfiguring webhooks. Estimating 2-6 weeks depending on how much of our Ably surface is actually call-shaped. I'll confirm details from each vendor's official docs before locking the plan.

Let me know if you want to review use-case fit first.

Thanks
